question.text: TVPI (Total Value to Paid-In) is calculated as:

explanation.text: **The Logic:** TVPI = (Distributions + Residual Value) / Paid-In Capital = DPI + RVPI. It measures the total value created per rupee invested, combining both realized (distributions) and unrealized (residual portfolio value) returns. **The Trap:** TVPI includes unrealized value (RVPI) which may change. A high TVPI driven primarily by RVPI is less certain than one driven by DPI (actual cash returned). **Cross-Exam Context:** TVPI and its components (DPI, RVPI) are core AIF metrics tested across all XIX-series exams. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** When comparing funds, look at TVPI composition. A 2.0x TVPI with DPI of 1.5x is better than 2.0x with DPI of 0.5x — the former has already returned most value in cash.
